### File: outputs/AWS/aws-certified-developer-associate-DVA-C01/21_AWS Serverless_ Lambda/310_Lambda Limits.txt

Line 1:
- Concepts: Operational Guardrails, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: So you need to know a few Lambda Limits before going into the exam, because the exam likes to see if you know these limits and these limits are per region.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Constraint or limitation signal.

Line 2:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Operational Guardrails
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So we have some execution limits and then we'll have some deployment limits.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Constraint or limitation signal; Runtime and execution-path signal; Platform automation signal.

Line 3:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So execution is that the memory allocation is between 128 megabytes to 10 gigabytes.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 4:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And this is in 64 megabytes increments.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 5:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And when we increase the memory, then we have more vCPU, you understand this now.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 6:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: The maximum execution time is 900 seconds which is 15 minutes.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 7:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: So anything above that is not a good use case for Lambda.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 8:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Environment variables, we can only have up to four kilobytes of environment variable, so a little bit amount of space.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Constraint or limitation signal.

Line 9:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: But there is a temp space if you want to pull in some big files while we create another function.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 10:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And so this is this capacity in the /tmp folder and we have up to 10 gigabytes there.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 11:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: We can have up to 1000 concurrent executions over Lambda functions.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 12:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: This can be increased as well if we do a request.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 13:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: But it's good to use reserved concurrency early on.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 14:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Finally, for deployments, the max size for your compressed zip is 50, uncompressed, it is 250 megabytes.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 15:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So anything above that, for example, big files, you should use a /tmp space instead.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal.

Line 16:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So as I said, in case you need to have a big file (mumbles), please use that directory.

Line 17:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And finally, again, the environment variables is four kilobytes.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 18:

### File: outputs/AWS/aws-certified-developer-associate-DVA-C01/21_AWS Serverless_ Lambda/311_Lambda Best Practices.txt

Line 1:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: So we have learned a lot on Lambda in this section and that was necessary going to the exam.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 2:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: Amazon RDS
- Key Insights: So just some final words of wisdom before we leave you.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 3:
- Concepts: Operational Guardrails
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So best practices.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal.

Line 4:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: You need to perform heavy duty work outside of your function handler to minimize the amount of time your handler will run for.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 5:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So that means that you need to connect your databases outside of your function handler, initialize the AWS SDK outside of your handler again and building any dependencies or datasets, again, outside of your function handler.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 6:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: You should use environment variables for anything that is going to be changing over time.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal.

Line 7:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: Amazon S3
- Key Insights: So your database connection strings, your S3 bucket name, et cetera, et cetera.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 8:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Do not put these values in your code.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 9:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And for password and sensitive values, you can encrypt these environment variables with KMS.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 10:
- Concepts: Compute Platform Design, Deployment Automation
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Next, you should minimize your deployment package size to its runtime necessities.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal; Runtime and execution-path signal; Platform automation signal.

Line 11:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So that means that if your function is too big, please break it down.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 12:
- Concepts: Operational Guardrails
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Also, remember the Lamda limits for your package size and so on.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Constraint or limitation signal.

Line 13:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: And if you need to reuse some libraries, think of leveraging Lambda layers.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 14:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And finally, avoid recursing code.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 15:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: Okay, never have a Lambda function call itself.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal.

Line 16:

- Key Insights: We can have the client directly invoke the Lambda function, but that means that the client would need IAM permissions, or we've seen we can use an application load balancer to have it in between the client and the Lambda function and that would expose our Lambda function as an HTTP endpoint.

Line 49:

Line 50:

Line 62:

Line 68:

### File: outputs/AWS/aws-certified-developer-associate-DVA-C01/23_AWS Serverless_ API Gateway/340_API Gateway Stages and Deployment.txt

Line 1:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway
- Key Insights: So we have deployed our first API through the API Gateway in a deployment stage.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 2:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway
- Key Insights: So what we've seen is that whenever we made changes to the API Gateway, they were not effective until we did a deployment.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal; Platform automation signal.

Line 3:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So we need to make a deployment for them to be in effect.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 4:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And it's a common source of confusion.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 5:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Some people will go and change how the API works, but forget to do a deployment and therefore the API will not be live.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 6:
- Concepts: Compute Platform Design
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So these changes are deployed to stages and you can have as many stages as you want and you can use the naming you want.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 7:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: For example, you can use a dev, test, and prod stage or a v1, v2, v3 whatever you want to do.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 8:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS Config
- Key Insights: And each stage will get its own configuration parameters and they can be rolled back seamlessly because a whole history of all the deployments that have happened onto a stage is kept.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 9:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So let's take the example of we have two stages and we're creating a breaking API change.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 10:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway, AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: So our Lambda function backing our API Gateway is going to change.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 11:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So we have the v1 stage and it invokes the v1 function and we've deployed it and so our v1 clients are going to be able to access our API through this URL.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 12:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: But we are programming a new version of our Lambda function called v2.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 13:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: And this v2 does not respect the same format of data and so on and so we know that if we deploy the changes on the v1 stage, it will break our v1 clients.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 14:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So what we do is then instead we decide to create a new stage, call it the v2 stage, pointing to the v2 function.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 15:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And this will create a new URL.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 16:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So now the api.example.com/v2, which represents the v2 stage.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 17:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And the cool thing is that our clients can now be updated to version two and access this new URL, and we can maintain compatibility.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 18:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So for a while, the v1 and the v2 can coexist together as two different stages.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 19:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway
- Key Insights: We can migrate the clients from the version one to the version two and then when no more clients are using the version one, then we can shut it down and move on with our API Gateway.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 20:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So this is a very common use cases for stages.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 21:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now, how do we expand this?
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 22:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: We can use stage variables.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 23:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway
- Key Insights: So stage variables are just like environment variables, but for the API Gateway stages.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 24:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: AWS Config
- Key Insights: And you use them to change configuration values without redeploying your API.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 25:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: So they can be used, for example, in the Lambda function ARN, for the HTTP endpoint, for your parameter mapping templates.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 26:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And so you can change pretty much anything through stage variables.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 27:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: AWS Config
- Key Insights: The use cases is to automatically configure the HTTP endpoint your stages talk to.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 28:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Config, AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: Would it be dev, test, and prod, or to pass configuration parameters to Lambda function through mapping templates or to point to the right Lambda function, as we'll see in a second.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 29:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: And so these stage variables will be passed to the contact object in your Lambda function so we can log it and get access to the value of the stage variables directly from our Lambda functions.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 30:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway
- Key Insights: Now, the format to access the value of a stage variable within API Gateway is $stageVariables with an S .variableName.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 31:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So here is a very, very common use cases for your stage variables.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 32:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway, AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: So we are going to create a stage variable that will indicate the corresponding Lambda alias that our API Gateway should invoke.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal; Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 33:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway, AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: And so automatically the API Gateway will invoke the right Lambda function.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 34:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So here is an example.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 35:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: We have the dev stage pointing to the dev alias and the dev alias is itself pointing 100% of the traffic to the latest Lambda version.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 36:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: We have a test stage pointing to the test alias of Lambda function pointing to the v2 version of our Lambda function.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 37:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And finally, the prod alias is linked to the prod stage and pointing as well, but this time, 95% on the v1 and 5% on the v2.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 38:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway, AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: So in this case we can do a Lambda alias change in the backend by updating the percentage on different versions, but we don't need to update the API Gateway.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 39:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: Each stage points to the right alias and each alias will be redirecting to the right Lambda function.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 40:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway
- Key Insights: So this is a very common pattern for the API Gateway and one we'll be re-practicing in the next lecture.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

### File: outputs/AWS/aws-certified-developer-associate-DVA-C01/23_AWS Serverless_ API Gateway/343_API Gateway Canary Deployments.txt

Line 1:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway
- Key Insights: So now let's talk about a way to perform canary deployments on your API gateway.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 2:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway
- Key Insights: So the idea is that you want to enable a way to test a small amount of traffic on the change you have done on your API gateway.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 3:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So usually it's done in production.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 4:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway, AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: So you would choose the amount of percent of traffic that the canary channel will receive and this would allow you to test your new version, for example of your API gateway, over the backend Lambda function or something like this.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 5:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So say our prod stage currently points to version one and our client wants to be able to test a new version.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 6:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So we're going to create a prod stage canary for version two and what this will do is that we will be allowing our client to have for example 95% of the traffic that goes to the existing prod stage which we know works and behind the scenes automatically 5% of the traffic will go to the canary stage.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 7:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: From this, we can test all the changes, look at metrics, look at logs, look at everything, maybe debug see if everything is working, and when we're confident, we can just move over 100% of the traffic into your canary stage.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 8:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So in this example, the metrics and the logs will be separate, so this is for better monitoring.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 9:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway, AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: And you can override any stage variables you wanted for your canary stage and this is equivalent of performing blue/green deployment with Lambda and API gateway.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 10:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So let's go into hands-on to see how that works.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 11:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model

Line 12:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model

### File: outputs/AWS/aws-certified-developer-associate-DVA-C01/23_AWS Serverless_ API Gateway/349_API Gateway Caching.txt

Line 1:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway
- Key Insights: So now let's talk about how we can enable caching of our responses in the API gateway.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 2:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So what is caching?
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 3:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Well, we know it.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 4:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Caching is to reduce the number of calls made into the backend.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 5:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway
- Key Insights: So that means that our clients, when they talk to the API gateway, the API gateway will first check the cache and if a result is already in cache, return that result.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 6:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Otherwise, if there's a cache miss, then it will go and talk to the backend to get the response.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 7:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So the idea is that we really leverage a lot of the cache to reduce the amount of pressure on our backend.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 8:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So the default TTL, which is how long the results live in the cache is 300 seconds.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 9:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So five minutes, but the min is zero, that means no caching and the max is one hour.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 10:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: The caches are defined at the stage level.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 11:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So you define one cache per stage, and it is possible for you to override the cache settings per method.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 12:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So if you want a method to not use a cache, you can specify it as well at the method level.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 13:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: The cache can be encrypted and the cache size is between 0.5 gigabytes, all the way to 237 gigabytes.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 14:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: The cache is really expensive, so it makes sense only if you're in production but it may not make sense if you're in development or test environment, for example, because there's not that many requests done on your backend anyway.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Constraint or limitation signal.

Line 15:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So caching is really something you wanna use in production or in pre-production.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 16:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Now, how do we invalidate the cache?
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 17:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway
- Key Insights: So you are able to invalidate the entire cache immediately from the UI, or clients can invalidate the cache with the header placed into a query to the API gateway called Cache-Control:max-age equals zero.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 18:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And for the clients to do this, they need proper IAM authorization.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 19:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So this is an IAM policy that would allow a client to invalidate the cache on a specific resource.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 20:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And if you don't impose an InvalidateCache policy or if you don't require authorization in the console, then any clients can invalidate the API cache and that could be a disaster.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 21:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So now you know everything about caching.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 22:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context

Line 4:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context

Line 5:
- Concepts: Compute Platform Design, Serverless Execution Model

Line 6:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway

Line 7:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway, AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: So let's take an example, the API gateway is deployed into a stage and secured with IAM permissions, and there's a backend of Lambda functions.

Line 8:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway, AWS Lambda, Amazon ECR
- Key Insights: So first our clients is going to do a rest API call and we'll pass the Sig v4 headers, then API gateway knows how to decrypt those, so it will check with IAM if the user is authorized and so there will be an IAM policy check, and so as soon as this is done the API gateway will, if it's authorized, talk to the Lambda function in the backend and returned a result to the clients.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 9:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 10:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context

Line 11:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway, AWS Lambda, AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So, resource policies sound a lot like Lambda resource policies because they are for the exact same purpose, they allow you to set adjacent policy on your API gateway to define who and what can access your API gateway.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 12:
- Concepts: Operational Guardrails, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway
- Key Insights: So the main use case of resource policies is to use cross account access, so we would define adjacent policy just like this and combined it with IAM security, we are able to give access of users or roles in other accounts directly into our API gateway.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 13:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 14:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So there is the first category of security.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 15:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: The second category is around Cognito user pool, so we'll see Cognito in its own section, but Cognito at a high level It's a database of users.

Line 16:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway
- Key Insights: So Cognito will manage fully the user lifecycle and the token of connection to Cognito will expire automatically, and so the API Gateway will verify the identity of the people connecting to API with Cognito.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 17:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway
- Key Insights: There's no custom implementation required, so in this case the users have to authenticate with the Cognito User Pools, and then the authorization is set at the API gateway methods level.

Line 18:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway, AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: So, if we have a look here our API gateway is talking to Lambda function in our backend, and then, our client first authenticates with the Cognito User Pool to retrieve a connection token.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 19:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway
- Key Insights: So the users are already restarting user pool, then our client is authenticated they received a token and it will pass the token in the API call to our API gateway.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 20:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway
- Key Insights: Now what will happen is that the API gateway has a direct integration with Cognito User Pools, and it will evaluate the Cognito token with the Cognito User Pool and if the token is correct, it's going to allow access to your backend.

Line 21:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context

Line 23:

### File: outputs/AWS/aws-certified-developer-associate-DVA-C01/25_AWS Serverless_ SAM - Serverless Application Model/377_SAM Overview.txt

Line 1:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So now let's talk about AWS SAM.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 2:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So SAM is this little squirrel, but it's also the Serverless Application Model.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 3:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: It's an actual framework to develop and deploy serverless applications.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 4:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS Config, AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So that means that you write your code and then you're going to use a configuration in the YAML format, which is going to be compliant with the SAM framework.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 5:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS CloudFormation, AWS SAM, Amazon Lex
- Key Insights: And automatically, SAM is going to generate very complex CloudFormation files from simple SAM YAML files.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 6:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS CloudFormation
- Key Insights: It supports anything from CloudFormation.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 7:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So you can still use outputs, mappings, parameter, resources, et cetera in your SAM YAML code.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 8:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway, AWS Lambda, AWS SAM, Amazon DynamoDB
- Key Insights: Now, SAM behind the scene can also use CodeDeploy to deploy Lambda functions, and SAM can help you run Lambda, API Gateway, and DynamoDB locally.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 9:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS CloudFormation, AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So SAM really is intended to be focused on serverless applications and allow you to debug them locally and to deploy them very quickly using CloudFormation into the AWS cloud.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 10:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So SAM is made of recipes, and at the very top of your template, you're going to add a transform header to indicate it's a SAM template.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 11:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS CloudFormation
- Key Insights: And this is a transform header which is going to tell CloudFormation to transform it into a CloudFormation template.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 12:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway, AWS CloudFormation, AWS Lambda, AWS SAM, Amazon DynamoDB
- Key Insights: Then you write your code, but instead of using the CloudFormation constructs, you're going to use the SAM constructs, such as a serverless function, which is a Lambda function, a serverless API, which is going to be the API gateway, and the serverless SimpleTable, which is going to be DynamoDB.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 13:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: But you have these constructs to make it simpler and easier for you to write your serverless applications.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 14:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: And then to package and deploy this application onto AWS, you're going to use the sam deploy command.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 15:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And it used to be two commands.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 16:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: It used to be sam package and then sam deploy.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 17:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: But now you can just do sam deploy on its own, and it is going to package it for you as well.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 18:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda, AWS SAM
- Key Insights: There's also a way to synchronize your changes into AWS Lambda very quickly using SAM Accelerate, and the command is sam sync and then minus minus watch.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 19:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: I'll give you a bit more information on this in this lecture.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 20:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So let's have a look into SAM deployment.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 21:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So you have your application code as well as your SAM template in the YAML format, and then you're going to build the application locally using sam build.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 22:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS CloudFormation, AWS SAM
- Key Insights: It's going to transform into a CloudFormation template and your application code, and then you can deploy it using sam deploy.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 23:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS CloudFormation, Amazon S3
- Key Insights: So you'll zip and upload everything into an S3 bucket, and then automatically execute or ChangeSet against CloudFormation.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 24:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway, AWS CloudFormation, AWS Lambda, Amazon DynamoDB
- Key Insights: Then your CloudFormation stack can be made of different serverless components such as Lambda, API Gateway, and DynamoDB.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 25:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So let's talk about SAM Accelerate.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 26:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So SAM Accelerate is its set of features that is used to reduce the latency while deploying resources to AWS.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 27:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So you want to deploy as fast as possible.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 28:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: And the command is going to be sam sync to declare your project, declare it with a SAM template into AWS.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 29:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS CloudFormation
- Key Insights: And it's actually going to bypass CloudFormation if you just do code changes without updating your infrastructure by using the service API.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 30:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So it's going to be a very, very quick deployment.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 31:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda, AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So you have SAM template and your application code, and your Lambda function already deployed and you're going to run sam sync.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 32:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda, AWS SAM
- Key Insights: And automatically, as you change the application code, automatically SAM is going to synchronize its code with your Lambda function.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 33:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: Therefore, if you wanted to test your Lambda function in the cloud, it's going to be very easy and very quick.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 34:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So different options, you can just run sam sync to synchronize the code and the infrastructure, or sam sync and just the code option to synchronize only the code and not the infrastructure.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Constraint or limitation signal; Platform automation signal.

Line 35:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS CloudFormation
- Key Insights: So you're not going to use CloudFormation, it's going to be like the updates will be done in seconds.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 36:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model

Line 37:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context

- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 38:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context

- Key Insights: That makes sense, right?

- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

### File: outputs/AWS/aws-certified-developer-associate-DVA-C01/25_AWS Serverless_ SAM - Serverless Application Model/381_SAM with CodeDeploy.txt

Line 1:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So here is something I promised you before.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 2:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda, AWS SAM
- Key Insights: We are going to see how CodeDeploy is integrated with the SAM framework, and SAM will be using CodeDeploy to update our Lambda functions, and that will leverage the traffic shifting feature we've seen before using the aliases.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 3:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda, Amazon CloudWatch
- Key Insights: We can also define pre and post-traffic hooks, Lambda functions to validate the deployments, and we can do an automated rollbacks with CloudWatch alarms.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 4:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda, AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So in a diagram to remember to remind you, we had the Lambda alias that had v1 Lambda, and we're going to trigger a CodeDeploy deployment whether with a CICD or with a SAM framework, and then we want to update our alias into a v2.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 5:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: So CodeDeploy will be running some pre-traffic hook test using another Lambda function, and that's optional.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 6:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda, Amazon CloudWatch
- Key Insights: Then it will do a traffic shifting with the alias, so according to your strategy, and then it will monitor a CloudWatch alarm, and that's also optional, to ensure everything goes well during the deployment, and then once the deployment is done and the traffic shifting is completely done, we can run a post-traffic hook Lambda function, this is again optional, to run some tests onto your alias, and if everything goes well, then your v1 function and your alias goes away and we're just ending up with the v2.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 7:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So here is an example of the YAML file that you will have within SAM.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 8:

### File: outputs/AWS/aws-certified-developer-associate-DVA-C01/25_AWS Serverless_ SAM - Serverless Application Model/382_SAM - Local Capabilities.txt

Line 1:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So now let's talk about the local capabilities of the SAM framework.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 2:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda, AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So by using the SAM framework, you can actually locally start AWS Lambda.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 3:
- Concepts: Compute Platform Design, Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda, AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So if you do SAM local start-Lambda, you will have your Lambda function available as a local endpoint on your computer with an emulation of the Lambda framework.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 4:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: The beautiful thing about it is that now you can start to run automated tests against these local endpoints.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 5:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: You can also locally invoke a Lambda function.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 6:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda, AWS SAM
- Key Insights: For this, you do SAM local invoke and this will actually invoke Lambda function with a payload.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal; Platform automation signal.

Line 7:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So you will do it once and then quit after the invocation is complete.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 8:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda, Amazon DynamoDB
- Key Insights: It's very helpful to generate test cases and in case your Lambda function is interacting with AWS because, for example, you're doing a API call to DynamoDB to fetch whatever, re whatever thing from it, then, of course, you must start your local function or invocate with the correct --profile option because you need to say against which environment you want to be running against.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal.

Line 9:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway
- Key Insights: You can also start a local API Gateway Endpoint.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 10:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: For this you do SAM local start-API and it's going to start a local HTTP server that will host all your APIs and all your functions.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 11:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: And if somehow you update your code for your Lambda function, automatically the functions are going to be reloaded and your API will be updated.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 12:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda, AWS SAM
- Key Insights: Finally, you can generate events for Lambda functions using the SAM local generate-event function.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 13:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM, Amazon S3
- Key Insights: And to do so, for example, you could combine it with, "Hey, I want to generate an event for Amazon S3 for putting in a bucket at a specific key," and then we pipe that input into the SAM local invoke command that we saw before.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal; Platform automation signal.

Line 14:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So you can generate sample payloads for event sources.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 15:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: It could be whatever source really is a source of Lambda.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 16:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: API Gateway, Amazon DynamoDB, Amazon Kinesis, Amazon S3
- Key Insights: So Amazon S3, API Gateway, SNS, Kinesis, DynamoDB, and pretty much all of them.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 17:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: Okay, so we've seen the local capabilities of using the SAM framework.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 18:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: I hope you liked it.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 19:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And I will see you in the next lecture.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

- Concepts: Deployment Automation

- Key Insights: So SAM allows you as well to easily manage multiple environments within your development stack.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 2:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So imagine that you're deploying sometimes your SAM templates into a dev environment and sometimes into a prod environment.

- Services: AWS Config, AWS SAM

Line 4:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And this file looks like this.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 5:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation

Line 10:

Line 11:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations, Serverless Execution Model

Line 12:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model

Line 13:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations, Serverless Execution Model

Line 14:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations

Line 15:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Batch, AWS Lambda, Amazon DynamoDB, Amazon ECS

- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 16:
- Concepts: Compute Platform Design, Container Runtime Operations, Serverless Execution Model

Line 17:

- Services: (none explicit)

### File: outputs/AWS/aws-certified-developer-associate-DVA-C01/28_Other Serverless_ Step Functions & AppSync/400_Step Functions - Error Handling.txt

Line 1:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So now let's talk about error handling in step functions and really define the whole power of step functions.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 2:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So a step function will execute many small tasks and these tasks will do very little amount of work.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 3:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: For example, talk to an API and so on.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 4:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: But all the error handling should happen outside of these tasks by step functions themselves.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal; Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 5:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And so when can we have errors for example?
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 6:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: Well, if the state machine has a definition issue, for example, there is no matching rule in the choice states, or if a task fails, for example, a Lambda function throws an exception, we should not catch it in the Lambda function itself, we should catch it in the step function error handling mechanism.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal; Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 7:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Or a transient failure.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 8:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: For example, there's a network partition events.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 9:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So we can have two types of error handling in step functions, a Retry to retry a task, or a Catch, to transition into a failure path.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 10:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And you should do this in the state machine instead of the application code.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal.

Line 11:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Because you make the application way simpler and you have all very nice mechanisms as well as execution history of these retries and these catch directly in the step function history.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 12:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So we have predefined error codes.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 13:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So States.All, too much any error name or State.Timeout, if the task ran longer than the timeout seconds or there's no heartbeat received from an activity.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 14:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Or TaskFailed in case of an execution failure of the task itself.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 15:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: For example, as I said, an exception in a Lambda function.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 16:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Or States.Permissions, because there is not enough permissions to execute some code.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 17:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So the state itself also may report its own errors and you can catch them in the step functions.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 18:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So let's talk about the Retry for tasks or parallel states.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 19:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So the Retry allows you to define what happens and how many times to retry based on some errors.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 20:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So this gets evaluated from top to bottom.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 21:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: And so we have here in this example a Lambda function being run, and we have one, two and three retry that are happening.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 22:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So the ErrorEquals match a specific kind of error.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 23:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: So here ErrorEquals Customerror, in case the Lambda function throws a customer error, or ErrorEquals TaskFailed, in case the Lambda function just fails but without throwing the custom error, or All to try to catch all kinds of errors that can happen within that Lambda function.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 24:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So interval seconds say, how long to wait before retrying?
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 25:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So in this example, one, so wait one second and retry it or this one 30 seconds, so wait 30 seconds and then retry it or this one, five seconds.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 26:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Then we have the BackoffRate, which is how many times you want to multiply the delay after retry.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 27:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: AWS SAM
- Key Insights: So to implement exponential backoff, so two, two, and two, in this example, they're all the same.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 28:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Then we have MaxAttempts, so how many times we should retry.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal.

Line 29:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So by default, it's three and zero, if you should never retry it, but in this example, we have MaxAttempts two, MaxAttempts two and MaxAttempts five.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal.

Line 30:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And then whenever all the attempts are fulfilled and reach, then the Catch block will kick in.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal.

Line 31:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: So in this example, as you can see, if you were to define all the Retry logic from within the Lambda function, we would make the Lambda function run for a very, very long time, obviously, and maybe would timeout.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 32:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: But also if you wanted to change your error handling logic you would have to redeploy Lambda function.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 33:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: But in this example, we are defining the retries from outside the Lambda function, into our step functions.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 34:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: Amazon Lex

Line 35:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model

- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 36:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context

Line 37:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And the Catch has a similar logic , it's evaluated to top to bottom and you have ErrorEquals and Next, so let's have a look.

Line 38:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: So in this example, we have this Lambda function and we're saying, okay, in case you find the ErrorEquals CustomError, then Next go to this state called CustomERrorFallback, which corresponds to the block right here.

Line 39:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context

Line 40:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 41:

### File: outputs/AWS/aws-certified-developer-associate-DVA-C01/28_Other Serverless_ Step Functions & AppSync/402_Step Functions - Wait For Task Token.txt

Line 1:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So let's talk about a step function feature called wait for task token.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 2:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So the idea is that you have your step functions and you have your execution workflow but then you want to wait for a task token to be returned because you're waiting for something external.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 3:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So you may be waiting for another AWS service to do its thing or human approval or a third-party integration or to call legacy systems.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 4:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And so to do so, you're going to, in your task in step functions, append the .waitForTaskToken to the resource field.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 5:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And this is going to tell step functions to wait for a specific task token to be returned before the execution can move on.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 6:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So for example, here we have a resource named sqs:sendMessage and then we waitForTaskToken.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 7:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: I will show you in the very next slide how that works.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 8:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So the task is going to be paused until it's going to receive a task token back with either a SendTaskSuccess if everything works or a SendTaskFailure API call.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 9:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So let's take an example of a workflow.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 10:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: We start, we're going to check the client credits and this actually relies on an external service.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 11:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So what do we do?
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 12:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: We are going to call SQS with a task token.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 13:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So we'll use the waitForTaskToken in the resource and then we pass in the input, of course, to the SQS queue in the message.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 14:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: But also, we're going to pass the task token so that the receiving application knows how to call back then step functions.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 15:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So then your SQS queues may be pulled by whatever application you may have.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 16:
- Concepts: Compute Platform Design, Container Runtime Operations, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda, Amazon EC2, Amazon ECS
- Key Insights: It could be Lambda functions, it could be ECS, it could be EC2, it could be a third-party server.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 17:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: It could be whatever you want, right?
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 18:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: But you're going to process the message.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 19:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And then, of course, you're going to get the message input body but also, you're going to get the task token.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 20:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And then if it's successful or a failure, you're going to use a SendTaskSuccess API call in which you're going to pass the outputs of the processing but also the task token that was passed initially to your SQS queue.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 21:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 22:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model

### File: outputs/AWS/aws-certified-developer-associate-DVA-C01/28_Other Serverless_ Step Functions & AppSync/403_Step Functions - Activity Tasks.txt

- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: (none explicit)

Line 4:
- Concepts: Compute Platform Design, Container Runtime Operations, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda, Amazon EC2
- Key Insights: So these activity workers, they may be running on EC2 instances, Lambda functions, mobile devices, whatever you want, and these activity workers are going to regularly pull for your step function's workflow to find a task.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 5:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And they will use the GetActivityTask API.

Line 6:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations, Deployment Automation, Serverless Execution Model

Line 7:
- Concepts: Compute Platform Design, Container Runtime Operations
- Services: Amazon EC2
- Key Insights: So here, the EC2 instance, for example, which is my activity worker, is going to be pulling for a task with a GetActivityTask, then step function says yes, there is something for you to do, actually.

Line 8:
- Concepts: Container Runtime Operations

### File: outputs/AWS/aws-certified-solutions-architect-associate-SAA-C03/19_Serverless Overviews from a Solution Architect Perspective/215_Lambda Limits.txt

Line 1:
- Concepts: Operational Guardrails, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: So you need to know a few Lambda Limits before going into the exam, because the exam likes to see if you know these limits and these limits are per region.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Constraint or limitation signal.

Line 2:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation, Operational Guardrails
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So we have some execution limits and then we'll have some deployment limits.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Constraint or limitation signal; Runtime and execution-path signal; Platform automation signal.

Line 3:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So execution is that the memory allocation is between 128 megabytes to 10 gigabytes.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 4:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And this is in 64 megabytes increments.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 5:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And when we increase the memory, then we have more vCPU, you understand this now.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 6:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: The maximum execution time is 900 seconds which is 15 minutes.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 7:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: So anything above that is not a good use case for Lambda.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 8:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Environment variables, we can only have up to four kilobytes of environment variable, so a little bit amount of space.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Constraint or limitation signal.

Line 9:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: But there is a temp space if you want to pull in some big files while we create another function.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 10:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And so this is this capacity in the /tmp folder and we have up to 10 gigabytes there.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 11:
- Concepts: Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: We can have up to 1000 concurrent executions over Lambda functions.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Runtime and execution-path signal.

Line 12:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: This can be increased as well if we do a request.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 13:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: But it's good to use reserved concurrency early on.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 14:
- Concepts: Deployment Automation
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: Finally, for deployments, the max size for your compressed zip is 50, uncompressed, it is 250 megabytes.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Platform automation signal.

Line 15:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So anything above that, for example, big files, you should use a /tmp space instead.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Operational guidance / best-practice signal.

Line 16:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: So as I said, in case you need to have a big file (mumbles), please use that directory.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 17:
- Concepts: Compute Operational Context
- Services: (none explicit)
- Key Insights: And finally, again, the environment variables is four kilobytes.
- Hidden/Implicit Meaning: Implicitly contributes compute behavior detail.

Line 18:
- Concepts: Operational Guardrails, Serverless Execution Model
- Services: AWS Lambda
- Key Insights: So once you know all these limits, when the exam will ask you a few questions such as we need 30 gigabytes of RAM, or we need 30 minutes of execution time, or we need a big file of three gigabytes, then you will know that Lambda is not the right way to run this workload.
